rows: May get wrong number of customer if exist customer without orders.

SELECT SUM(T1.TotalAmount) 
FROM Orders AS T1 
JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID 
WHERE YEAR(T1.OrderDate) = 2025
SELECT SUM(T1.TotalAmount) FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE STRFTIME('%Y', T1.OrderDate) = '2025'
Show the top 5 products by revenue.

SELECT TOP 5
    p.ProductID,
    p.ProductName,
    SUM(s.Quantity * p.Price) AS total_revenue
FROM Product p
JOIN OrderDetails s ON p.ProductID = s.ProductID
GROUP BY p.ProductID, p.ProductName
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC

Five rows sorted by revenue descending.
Car, iPhone, Laptop, Headphones, Pen.

SELECT Product.ProductName, SUM(Quantity * Price) AS TotalRevenue FROM OrderDetails JOIN Product ON OrderDetails.ProductID = Product.ProductID GROUP BY Product.ProductID ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC LIMIT 5;

Five rows sorted by revenue descending.
Car, iPhone, Laptop, Headphones, Pen.
What is the average order value by city? (subquery)

SELECT 
    c.City,
    AVG(CAST(ot.order_value AS FLOAT)) AS average_order_value
FROM Customer c
JOIN Orders o ON c.CustomerID = o.CustomerID
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        OrderID,
        SUM(Quantity * p.Price) AS order_value
    FROM OrderDetails od
    JOIN Product p ON od.ProductID = p.ProductID
    GROUP BY OrderID
) ot ON o.OrderID = ot.OrderID
GROUP BY c.City
ORDER BY average_order_value DESC;
4 cities in order: Montreal, Ottawa, Toronto, Vancouver
SELECT AVG(T1.TotalAmount) ,  T2.City FROM Orders AS T1 JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID GROUP BY T2.City
Check if the TotalAmount is equal to the total details (quantity * price) in each order

SELECT 
    COUNT(*) AS total_orders,
    SUM(CASE WHEN o.TotalAmount = calculated.order_total THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matching_orders,
    COUNT(*) - SUM(CASE WHEN o.TotalAmount = calculated.order_total THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS mismatch_count
FROM Orders o
JOIN (
    SELECT 
        OrderID,
        SUM(Quantity * p.Price) AS order_total
    FROM OrderDetails od
    JOIN Product p ON od.ProductID = p.ProductID
    GROUP BY OrderID
) calculated ON o.OrderID = calculated.OrderID;

SELECT 
    o.OrderID,
    CASE 
        WHEN SUM(od.Quantity * p.Price) = o.TotalAmount THEN 'Yes'
        ELSE 'No'
    END AS TotalAmountEqualsTotalDetails
FROM 
    Orders o
JOIN 
    OrderDetails od ON o.OrderID = od.OrderID
JOIN 
    Product p ON od.ProductID = p.ProductID
GROUP BY 
    o.OrderID
Check is correct as there is discount info, that's qhy they are mismatch.

SELECT DISTINCT T2.Name 
FROM Orders AS T1 
JOIN Customer AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID 
WHERE T1.TotalAmount > 100;

SELECT DISTINCT C.Name 
FROM Customer C 
JOIN Orders O ON C.CustomerID = O.CustomerID 
WHERE O.TotalAmount > 100.0;
